On it — asking Guru about registering MCP servers in Claude for Work now.

**Guru** — High confidence

## Registering a Custom MCP Server in Claude for Work (Teams/Enterprise)

### TL;DR
It is done entirely through the **claude.ai UI** — no config file involved. The entry point is **Organization settings → Connectors**.

---

### Step-by-Step (Owner role required)

1. **Go to Organization settings → Connectors** (only Owners/Primary Owners can do this on Team/Enterprise plans).
2. Click **"Add"** (or "Add custom connector").
3. Enter a **name** for your connector and the **remote MCP server URL** (e.g., `https://managed-agents-poc.onrender.com`).
4. Optionally click **"Advanced settings"** to add an **OAuth Client ID** and **OAuth Client Secret** if your server requires auth.
5. Click **"Add"** to finish.

Once an Owner registers the connector, it becomes available to the organization. Individual users then **opt in per-conversation** via the **"+" button → Connectors** in the chat interface — toggling it on/off as needed.

---

### Critical Requirements for Your Remote Server

- Your MCP server **must be publicly reachable over the internet** — Anthropic's servers (not the user's machine) initiate the connection.
- Servers behind a VPN, private network, or firewall will **not work**.
- Your Render-hosted backend at `https://managed-agents-poc.onrender.com` should satisfy this requirement as long as it is publicly accessible and implements the **MCP HTTP (SSE or Streamable HTTP) transport** — not stdio.

---

### Plans Supported
Custom connectors via remote MCP are available on **Free, Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise** plans. On Team/Enterprise, only Owners can register them org-wide.

---

### This is Different From Claude Code CLI
Claude Code uses a local `~/.claude/claude_desktop_config.json` (or project `.claude/mcp.json`) and supports **stdio** subprocesses. Claude for Work on claude.ai only supports **remote HTTP-based MCP servers** via the UI Connectors settings.

**Sources:**
1. [Get started with custom connectors using remote MCP | Claude Help Center](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11175166-get-started-with-custom-connectors-using-remote-mcp) — 2025
2. [Build custom connectors via remote MCP servers | Claude Help Center](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11503834-build-custom-connectors-via-remote-mcp-servers) — 2025
3. [Use connectors to extend Claude's capabilities | Claude Help Center](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11176164-use-connectors-to-extend-claude-s-capabilities) — 2025
4. [Custom Connectors | Claude Help Center](https://support.claude.com/en/collections/17879441-custom-connectors) — 2025

**Caveats:** Your MCP server must implement remote HTTP transport (SSE or Streamable HTTP) — stdio subprocess mode used in Claude Code CLI is NOT supported in the claude.ai web UI connector system.; On Team/Enterprise plans, only Owners (not regular admins or members) can register connectors at the org level. Individual users on Free/Pro can add their own at claude.ai/settings/connectors.; Anthropic's servers connect TO your server — so it must be publicly internet-accessible. Your Render deployment satisfies this as long as the endpoint is not blocked.; OAuth support is optional — if your server does not require auth, you can skip the Advanced settings step.; The connection originates from Anthropic's infrastructure, so you may need to whitelist Anthropic IP ranges on your server if you add IP-based firewall rules in the future.
Agent — rate limited, retrying...

Guru has been consulted — the response should appear in the app. While that loads, here's the key issue to be aware of: your current `flow-manager` MCP server uses **stdio transport** (subprocess), which works with Claude Code CLI but not Claude.ai web. Claude for Work requires MCP servers to expose an **HTTP/SSE endpoint** instead. That would likely require a code change to add an HTTP transport layer to the MCP server pointing at your Render backend.
